title: Downstream C API
code: library(Rminibwa)  td <- tempfile("rminibwa-capi-") dir.create(td) ref <- paste(rep("ACGT", 1000), collapse = "") fa <- file.path(td, "ref.fa") writeLines(c(">chr1", ref), fa, useBytes = TRUE) prefix <- file.path(td, "idx")  mb_index_build(fa, prefix, threads = 1L) idx <- mb_index_load(prefix) aln <- charToRaw(substr(ref, 1L, 100L)) |>   mb_map(idx, opt = mb_opts("sr", out_n = 0L), name = charToRaw("read1"))  mb_align_n(aln) #> [1] 51 mb_align_col(aln, "tid")[[1]] #> [1] 0

title: Downstream C API
code: #define _Complex #include <limits.h> #include <stdint.h> #include <stddef.h> #include <R.h> #include <Rinternals.h> #include <Rminibwa.h>  static int clamp_size_to_int(size_t x) {     return x > (size_t) INT_MAX ? INT_MAX : (int) x; }  SEXP rminibwa_capi_summary(SEXP align_x) {     const RmbAlignBatch *batch = Rminibwa_align_from_sexp(align_x);     const size_t n = Rminibwa_align_n(batch);     const int32_t *tid = Rminibwa_align_i32_col(batch, "tid");     const int32_t *qe = Rminibwa_align_i32_col(batch, "qe");     const int64_t *ts = Rminibwa_align_i64_col(batch, "ts");     size_t n_cigar_words = 0;     (void) Rminibwa_align_cigar_words(batch, &n_cigar_words);      SEXP out = PROTECT(Rf_allocVector(INTSXP, 4));     INTEGER(out)[0] = clamp_size_to_int(n);     INTEGER(out)[1] = n && tid ? tid[0] : NA_INTEGER;     INTEGER(out)[2] = n && qe ? qe[0] : NA_INTEGER;     INTEGER(out)[3] = clamp_size_to_int(n_cigar_words);      SEXP names = PROTECT(Rf_allocVector(STRSXP, 4));     SET_STRING_ELT(names, 0, Rf_mkChar("n"));     SET_STRING_ELT(names, 1, Rf_mkChar("first_tid"));     SET_STRING_ELT(names, 2, Rf_mkChar("first_qe"));     SET_STRING_ELT(names, 3, Rf_mkChar("cigar_words"));     Rf_setAttrib(out, R_NamesSymbol, names);      /* Touch one 64-bit column so this example covers both integer-width paths. */     if (n && ts && ts[0] < 0) INTEGER(out)[2] = NA_INTEGER;      UNPROTECT(2);     return out; }

what: Compile and call it with Rtinycc
title: Downstream C API
text: C function never asks R materialize data frame. receives batch SEXP, obtains opaque RmbAlignBatch *, reads borrowed int32_t, int64_t, packed CIGAR buffers directly.
code: ffi <- Rtinycc::tcc_ffi() |>   Rtinycc::tcc_include(system.file("include", package = "Rminibwa")) |>   Rtinycc::tcc_source(rminibwa_capi_code) |>   Rtinycc::tcc_bind(     rminibwa_capi_summary = list(args = list("sexp"), returns = "sexp")   ) |>   Rtinycc::tcc_compile()  ffi$rminibwa_capi_summary(aln) #>           n   first_tid    first_qe cigar_words  #>          51           0         100          51

title: SIMDe Dispatch Design
text: Dispatch starts KSW functions called minibwa: ksw_extz2_sse(); ksw_extd2_sse(); ksw_ll_qinit() ksw_ll_* helpers. rest minibwa stays baseline-compiled : index loading; BWT seeding; chaining; pairing; formatting; R external-pointer glue.

title: SIMDe Dispatch Design
text: Rminibwa currently exposes: scalar: portable SIMDe fallback compiled native intrinsics disabled ISA flags; sse4: native SSE4.1/SIMDe-alias build KSW files; avx2: native AVX2 build, including widened dual-gap extd2 kernel. avx2 backend uses wide ksw2_extd2_wide.c patch dominant dual-gap gap-filling path uses AVX2-compiled KSW objects remaining KSW entry points.

title: SIMDe Dispatch Design
text: Select backend explicitly diagnostics benchmarks: simd_set_backend() errors requested backend compiled supported current CPU/runtime.
code: library(Rminibwa) simd_info() #> $dispatch_mode #> [1] "rminibwa-ksw-staged" #>  #> $requested_backend #> [1] "auto" #>  #> $selected_backend #> [1] "avx2" #>  #> $compiled_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse4"   "avx2"   #>  #> $cpu_supported_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse4"   "avx2"   #>  #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse4"   "avx2"   #>  #> $target #> [1] "x86_64" old <- simd_backend() simd_set_backend("scalar") simd_backend() #> [1] "scalar" simd_set_backend("auto")

title: SIMDe Dispatch Design
text: every available backend, Rminibwa runs native mapping fixture tinytest checks native alignment batch ALTREP column accessors remain valid. README make rdm path additionally benchmarks: internal Rminibwa-scalar vs sse4 vs avx2; Rminibwa AVX2 vs locally built Python/Rust bindings compiled native AVX2 codegen. Backend timing must use workload actually reaches KSW. short exact matches can resolved seeding/chaining ungapped fast paths without single ksw_ext* ksw_ll* call, apparent scalar/sse4/avx2 differences examples just end--end mapper noise. developer README benchmark uses indel-mutated random read records internal KSW call counters timing count-mapping.

title: Vendoring minibwa
text: upstream lock file tools/minibwa-upstream.dcf: records component name, upstream version, repository, commit, archive URL, patch directory, license note.
code: Rscript tools/vendor-minibwa.R status

title: Vendoring minibwa
text: Patches live tools/patches/minibwa/ applied lexical order. Keep patches small tied exact pinned commit. current patch adds opt-RMINIBWA_USE_SIMDE branch KSW include blocks. change upstream behavior unless macro defined.

title: Vendoring minibwa
text: Fetch pinned upstream source .sync/minibwa: Build local developer CLI SIMDe patch queue applied: default developer build uses: gpl=0, avoiding optional GPL components; mimalloc=0, using ordinary allocation simpler local debugging; omp=0, avoiding OpenMP requirements package work; RsimdDispatch’s installed SIMDe headers.
code: tools/fetch-minibwa.sh tools/build-minibwa-cli.sh export RMINIBWA_MINIBWA=$PWD/.local/bin/minibwa

title: Vendoring minibwa
text: native bindings begin, stage pinned source src/vendor/minibwa: script downloads pinned GitHub archive, unpacks , applies patch queue, verifies MB_VERSION, writes src/vendor/minibwa/RMINIBWA_VENDOR.dcf archive checksum patch metadata. Use clean remove staged source: silently edit vendored upstream C. Update patch queue rerun vendor script instead.
code: Rscript tools/vendor-minibwa.R refresh Rscript tools/vendor-minibwa.R clean

what: Native R Bindings and SIMD Dispatch for minibwa
title: Native R Bindings and SIMD Dispatch for minibwa
text: Rminibwa R-facing package integrating minibwa, Heng Li’s short-read aligner, R. package starts command-line wrappers keeps pinned upstream source patch queue native-library work. native path follows RsimdDispatch/SIMDe style: use upstream minibwa algorithms, isolate SIMD-sensitive KSW kernels, stage backend objects configure time, select portable scalar, sse4, avx2 backends runtime.

title: Native R Bindings and SIMD Dispatch for minibwa
text: CLI wrappers use installed minibwa executable runtime. native C-library backend uses GNU make zlib. Set custom CLI binary :
code: repos <- c(   sounkou = "https://sounkou-bioinfo.r-universe.dev",   CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org" ) install.packages("RsimdDispatch", repos = repos)  # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("sounkou-bioinfo/Rminibwa", repos = repos) Sys.setenv(RMINIBWA_MINIBWA = "/path/to/minibwa")

title: Native R Bindings and SIMD Dispatch for minibwa
text: minibwa_map() returns captured output output = NULL writes file output supplied.
code: library(Rminibwa)  minibwa_available() minibwa_version()  prefix <- minibwa_index("ref.fa", threads = 8) aln <- minibwa_map(prefix, "reads.fq.gz", format = "paf", threads = 8)

title: Native R Bindings and SIMD Dispatch for minibwa
text: native path keeps hot alignment shape native columnar batch. R gets raw-byte inputs ALTREP column views; downstream packages can consume batch inst/include/Rminibwa.h without copying data frame.
code: library(Rminibwa)  td <- tempfile("rminibwa-readme-") dir.create(td) ref <- paste(rep("ACGT", 1000), collapse = "") fa <- file.path(td, "ref.fa") writeLines(c(">chr1", ref), fa, useBytes = TRUE) prefix <- file.path(td, "idx")  mb_index_build(fa, prefix, threads = 1L) idx <- mb_index_load(prefix) aln <- charToRaw(substr(ref, 1L, 100L)) |>   mb_map(idx, opt = mb_opts("sr", out_n = 0L), name = charToRaw("read1"))  c(   n = mb_align_n(aln),   first_tid = mb_align_col(aln, "tid")[[1]],   first_qe = mb_align_col(aln, "qe")[[1]],   cigar_bytes = length(mb_align_cigar_words(aln)) ) #>           n   first_tid    first_qe cigar_bytes  #>          51           0         100         204

title: Native R Bindings and SIMD Dispatch for minibwa
text: Rminibwa.h installed downstream C consumers. inline C chunk uses R_GetCCallable() header wrappers, reads borrowed alignment columns directly. rtinycc knitr engine displays C code stores chunk body rminibwa_capi_code next R chunk.
code: #define _Complex #include <limits.h> #include <stdint.h> #include <stddef.h> #include <R.h> #include <Rinternals.h> #include <Rminibwa.h>  static int clamp_size_to_int(size_t x) {     return x > (size_t) INT_MAX ? INT_MAX : (int) x; }  SEXP rminibwa_capi_summary(SEXP align_x) {     const RmbAlignBatch *batch = Rminibwa_align_from_sexp(align_x);     const size_t n = Rminibwa_align_n(batch);     const int32_t *tid = Rminibwa_align_i32_col(batch, "tid");     const int32_t *qe = Rminibwa_align_i32_col(batch, "qe");     const int64_t *ts = Rminibwa_align_i64_col(batch, "ts");     size_t n_cigar_words = 0;     (void) Rminibwa_align_cigar_words(batch, &n_cigar_words);      SEXP out = PROTECT(Rf_allocVector(INTSXP, 4));     INTEGER(out)[0] = clamp_size_to_int(n);     INTEGER(out)[1] = n && tid ? tid[0] : NA_INTEGER;     INTEGER(out)[2] = n && qe ? qe[0] : NA_INTEGER;     INTEGER(out)[3] = clamp_size_to_int(n_cigar_words);      SEXP names = PROTECT(Rf_allocVector(STRSXP, 4));     SET_STRING_ELT(names, 0, Rf_mkChar("n"));     SET_STRING_ELT(names, 1, Rf_mkChar("first_tid"));     SET_STRING_ELT(names, 2, Rf_mkChar("first_qe"));     SET_STRING_ELT(names, 3, Rf_mkChar("cigar_words"));     Rf_setAttrib(out, R_NamesSymbol, names);      /* Touch one 64-bit column so this example covers both integer-width paths. */     if (n && ts && ts[0] < 0) INTEGER(out)[2] = NA_INTEGER;      UNPROTECT(2);     return out; } ffi <- Rtinycc::tcc_ffi() |>   Rtinycc::tcc_include(system.file("include", package = "Rminibwa")) |>   Rtinycc::tcc_source(rminibwa_capi_code) |>   Rtinycc::tcc_bind(     rminibwa_capi_summary = list(args = list("sexp"), returns = "sexp")   ) |>   Rtinycc::tcc_compile()  ffi$rminibwa_capi_summary(aln) #>           n   first_tid    first_qe cigar_words  #>          51           0         100          51

title: Native R Bindings and SIMD Dispatch for minibwa
text: make rdm renders section building local Python Rust bindings native/AVX2 codegen passing paths environment variables. workload deliberately uses random reference indel-mutated read; short exact-match toy read can bypass KSW entirely, making scalar, sse4, avx2 differences meaningless. first benchmark internal Rminibwa forces staged backends installed package. second benchmark separates Rminibwa’s count-mapper path full batch materialization comparing Python binding tiny Rust cdylib shim around Rust binding.
code: library(bench) library(reticulate)  bench_td <- tempfile("rminibwa-bench-") dir.create(bench_td) set.seed(1) bench_ref <- paste(sample(c("A", "C", "G", "T"), 100000, replace = TRUE), collapse = "") delete_every <- function(x, every = 25L) {   y <- strsplit(x, "", fixed = TRUE)[[1L]]   paste(y[-seq(every, length(y), by = every)], collapse = "") } bench_fa <- file.path(bench_td, "ref.fa") writeLines(c(">chr1", bench_ref), bench_fa, useBytes = TRUE) bench_prefix <- file.path(bench_td, "idx") mb_index_build(bench_fa, bench_prefix, threads = 1L) bench_idx <- mb_index_load(bench_prefix) bench_opt <- mb_opts("sr", out_n = 0L) bench_query <- delete_every(substr(bench_ref, 1000L, 5999L), every = 25L) bench_query_raw <- charToRaw(bench_query) bench_name_raw <- charToRaw("read1")  internal_backends <- intersect(c("scalar", "sse4", "avx2"), simd_info()$available_backends) ksw_counts <- do.call(rbind, lapply(internal_backends, function(backend) {   simd_set_backend(backend)   invisible(Rminibwa:::simd_counters(TRUE))   invisible(Rminibwa:::mb_map_count(bench_query_raw, bench_idx, bench_opt, bench_name_raw))   data.frame(backend = backend, as.list(Rminibwa:::simd_counters(FALSE)), check.names = FALSE) })) ksw_counts #>   backend extz2 extd2 ll_qinit ll_u8_core ll_i16_core ll_i16 #> 1  scalar     0   198        0          0           0      0 #> 2    sse4     0   198        0          0           0      0 #> 3    avx2     0   198        0          0           0      0  rminibwa_internal <- do.call(rbind, lapply(internal_backends, function(backend) {   simd_set_backend(backend)   out <- bench::mark(     count_only = Rminibwa:::mb_map_count(bench_query_raw, bench_idx, bench_opt, bench_name_raw),     iterations = 100,     check = FALSE,     time_unit = "us"   )   out$backend <- backend   out })) rminibwa_internal[, c("backend", "min", "median", "itr/sec", "mem_alloc")] #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   backend   min median `itr/sec` mem_alloc #>   <chr>   <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl> <bch:byt> #> 1 scalar   440.   470.     2141.        0B #> 2 sse4     409.   419.     2371.        0B #> 3 avx2     411.   431.     2297.        0B  py_path <- Sys.getenv("RMINIBWA_BENCH_PYTHONPATH") py_run_string(sprintf("import sys; sys.path.insert(0, %s)", shQuote(py_path))) py_minibwa <- import("minibwa", convert = FALSE) py_idx <- py_minibwa$Index$load(bench_prefix) py_opt <- py_minibwa$Opts("sr") invisible(py_opt$set_out_n(0L))  rust_lib <- Sys.getenv("RMINIBWA_BENCH_RUST_LIB") dyn.load(rust_lib) invisible(.C("rminibwa_bench_rust_init_c", bench_prefix))  simd_set_backend("avx2") external_compare <- bench::mark(   rminibwa_avx2_count = Rminibwa:::mb_map_count(bench_query_raw, bench_idx, bench_opt, bench_name_raw),   rminibwa_avx2_batch = mb_align_n(mb_map(bench_query_raw, bench_idx, bench_opt, bench_name_raw)),   python_pyo3 = length(py_minibwa$map(py_idx, py_opt, "read1", bench_query, "none")),   rust_cdylib = .C("rminibwa_bench_rust_map_count_c", bench_query, out = integer(1L))$out,   iterations = 100,   check = FALSE,   time_unit = "us" ) external_compare[, c("expression", "min", "median", "itr/sec", "mem_alloc")] #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   expression            min median `itr/sec` mem_alloc #>   <bch:expr>          <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl> <bch:byt> #> 1 rminibwa_avx2_count  427.   434.     2293.        0B #> 2 rminibwa_avx2_batch  432.   445.     2242.        0B #> 3 python_pyo3          564.   585.     1683.    4.68KB #> 4 rust_cdylib          421.   435.     2284.    4.74KB  invisible(.C("rminibwa_bench_rust_clear_c")) dyn.unload(rust_lib) simd_set_backend("auto") unlink(bench_td, recursive = TRUE)

title: Native R Bindings and SIMD Dispatch for minibwa

Build local developer CLI pinned commit SIMDe patch queue applied: Stage vendored source tree native bindings begin: vendoring script records upstream commit, archive checksum, applied patches src/vendor/minibwa/RMINIBWA_VENDOR.dcf.

title: Native R Bindings and SIMD Dispatch for minibwa
text: native implementation deliberately narrow: keep CLI wrapper compatibility oracle; load .l2b/.mbw indexes R external pointers; expose mb_map() .Call() raw-byte inputs native columnar batches; dispatch KSW alignment kernels staged RsimdDispatch/SIMDe-style runtime-selection pattern. See package vignettes details.

what: Inspect native minibwa alignment batches — mb_align_n
title: Inspect native minibwa alignment batches — mb_align_n

text: x Native alignment batch returned mb_map(). name Column name.

title: Inspect native minibwa alignment batches — mb_align_n
text: mb_align_n() returns number records. mb_align_col() returns ALTREP integer real vector. mb_align_cigar_words() returns raw debug copy packed minibwa CIGAR words.

text: output = NULL, captured alignment records character vector; otherwise output path, invisibly.

title: Locate the minibwa executable — minibwa_path
text: minibwa_path() resolves command-line executable used CLI-backed helpers. default first consults RMINIBWA_MINIBWA environment variable otherwise searches minibwa PATH.

title: Locate the minibwa executable — minibwa_path
code: minibwa_path(   path = Sys.getenv("RMINIBWA_MINIBWA", unset = "minibwa"),   must_work = TRUE )

title: Locate the minibwa executable — minibwa_path
text: path Executable name path. must_work TRUE, throw error executable found.

title: Locate the minibwa executable — minibwa_path
text: scalar character path, NA_character_ must_work = FALSE executable found.

what: Report the pinned upstream minibwa source — minibwa_upstream_info
title: Report the pinned upstream minibwa source — minibwa_upstream_info
text: Rminibwa pins specific upstream minibwa commit native-library work. minibwa_upstream_info() reports installed copy provenance metadata.

title: Report the pinned upstream minibwa source — minibwa_upstream_info
code: minibwa_upstream_info()

title: Report the pinned upstream minibwa source — minibwa_upstream_info
text: named list fields Component, Version, Repository, Commit, PatchDirectory.

title: Report the pinned upstream minibwa source — minibwa_upstream_info
code: minibwa_upstream_info()[c("Version", "Commit")] #> $Version #> [1] "0.2-r379-dirty" #>  #> $Commit #> [1] "f0fd108456d459e5da3755f29c8700dc7f6fe0df" #>

text: path Executable name path.

title: Report SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
text: Report SIMD dispatch diagnostics

what: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: Rminibwa compiles staged KSW backend objects switches among runtime. Use "auto" best available backend, force one "scalar", "sse4", "avx2" diagnostics benchmarks. "scalar" backend portable SIMDe baseline native intrinsics disabled; compilers may still lower portable vector types efficiently.

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
code: simd_set_backend(backend = "auto")

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: backend Character scalar backend name.

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: selected backend, invisibly.

title: Rminibwa 0.0.0.9000
text: Bootstrap Rminibwa GPL (>= 2) R package wrapping pinned upstream lh3/minibwa C sources. Add native bindings index build/load, raw-vector query mapping, native external-pointer alignment batches, ALTREP column views, packed CIGAR accessors. Install inst/include/Rminibwa.h register C-callable accessors downstream packages can consume alignment batches directly C. Add staged KSW SIMD dispatch following RsimdDispatch pattern: portable scalar/SIMDe fallback, SSE4, AVX2 backends compiled separate objects selected runtime without global ISA compiler flags. Add AVX2 dual-gap extd2 support minibwa binding work keep optional runtime-dispatched backend. Vendor minibwa commit f0fd108456d459e5da3755f29c8700dc7f6fe0df patch queue provenance metadata. Add Rtinycc inline downstream-C examples README, vignette, tests. Add developer-benchmarks comparing internal staged backends external Python/Rust bindings built native AVX2 flags. Add pkgdown configuration GitHub Actions workflows standard R CMD check, Fedora/R-devel CRAN-style check, GitHub Pages deployment.
